title: Run-length encode the input as count/byte pairs
prompt: Read all bytes from standard input. Walk left to right; for every maximal run of identical consecutive bytes, write one line of the form `<count> <byte_decimal>\n` to standard output where `<count>` is the run length in decimal and `<byte_decimal>` is the byte value (0..255) in decimal. The lines must appear in input order. Empty input produces empty output. Exit with status 0.

input_schema.stdin: <input bytes, possibly empty, up to 1000 bytes>

output_schema.stdout: <zero or more lines of '<count> <byte_decimal>\n' in input order>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads the input bytes from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; the bytes are interpreted identically to stdin reads in the other languages. All other languages read all bytes from stdin until EOF. The harness adapts per language.
